issues.title: Toast notifications lack ARIA live region and role attributes
issues.description: The ToastEntry component renders toast notifications without role='alert' or aria-live='assertive' attributes. Screen readers cannot announce toast messages. The dismiss button also lacks an accessible label.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ToastEntry function component, make these specific changes: Find the outermost container div of each toast notification. BEFORE: The toast div has no ARIA attributes, so screen readers ignore it entirely. The dismiss/close button has no aria-label. AFTER: Add role='alert' and aria-live='assertive' to the toast container div. Add aria-label='Dismiss notification' to the dismiss/close button element. If the toast has an action button, ensure it has aria-label={toast.action.label}. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: FolderBreadcrumbs truncation incomplete and missing touch targets
issues.description: The FolderBreadcrumbs component starts mobile detection logic but the code is truncated. The crumbButtonStyle sets minHeight/minWidth to 44px (good), but the chevron separator between crumbs has no touch padding, making breadcrumb navigation difficult on mobile devices.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, in the FolderBreadcrumbs component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The ChevronRight separator icon between breadcrumb items is rendered inline with no wrapper or padding, making it a tiny touch target. The mobile collapsed state (when expanded is false and isMobile is true and stack.length > 2) does not render an ellipsis button to expand hidden crumbs. AFTER: Wrap each ChevronRight separator in a span with style={{ display: 'inline-flex', alignItems: 'center', padding: '4px', minWidth: '24px', minHeight: '44px', justifyContent: 'center' }} and set aria-hidden='true' on the span. When isMobile is true and stack.length > 2 and expanded is false, render only the first crumb, an ellipsis button (styled with minHeight 44px and minWidth 44px, aria-label='Show full path', onClick={() => setExpanded(true)}), and the last crumb. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Error toasts auto-dismiss too aggressively, no persistent error state
issues.description: Error toasts have TOAST_DURATION set to null but a fallback auto-dismiss at 30 seconds. For critical API failures or permission errors, 30 seconds may not be enough. The user might miss the error entirely if they step away briefly. Error toasts should persist until manually dismissed.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ToastEntry component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: There is a useEffect that auto-dismisses error toasts after 30000ms (30 seconds) as a fallback. AFTER: Change the error toast fallback timeout from 30000ms to 60000ms (60 seconds). Add a visible countdown indicator to the error toast: render a small text element showing 'Dismisses in Xs' that decrements every second, placed after the dismiss button. This gives users clear feedback that the toast will eventually disappear while keeping the error visible longer. Keep the pause-on-hover behavior for error toasts as well by integrating with the existing handleMouseEnter/handleMouseLeave pause logic. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Breadcrumbs route labels missing for key construction pages
issues.description: The routeLabels map is missing entries for 'warranties', 'equipment', 'portfolio', and 'photos' routes. When users navigate to these pages, the breadcrumb falls back to showing the raw URL segment (e.g., 'pay-apps' shows correctly but 'warranties' would show as raw slug). Construction professionals expect proper labels.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, in the routeLabels constant object, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The routeLabels Record only has entries for the routes listed in the current code (dashboard through bim). Routes like 'warranties', 'equipment', 'portfolio', 'photos', 'prevailing-wage', 'workforce', and 'deliveries' are missing. AFTER: Add these entries to the routeLabels object: 'warranties': 'Warranties', 'equipment': 'Equipment', 'portfolio': 'Portfolio', 'photos': 'Photos', 'prevailing-wage': 'Prevailing Wage', 'workforce': 'Workforce', 'deliveries': 'Deliveries', 'notifications': 'Notifications', 'profile': 'Profile'. Also add a fallback in the breadcrumb rendering logic: if a path segment is not found in routeLabels, transform it by replacing hyphens with spaces and capitalizing each word (e.g., 'some-new-page' becomes 'Some New Page') instead of showing the raw slug. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Toast action buttons have no keyboard focus styles or Enter key handling
issues.description: Toast action buttons (e.g., 'Undo' after bulk operations) lack visible focus ring styles for keyboard navigation. The design standards require 2px ring in primary color on focus. Additionally, the toast dismiss button does not respond to Enter or Space key presses if only onClick is wired.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ToastEntry component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The toast action button and dismiss button use inline styles but have no :focus-visible equivalent (since inline styles cannot use pseudo-selectors). There is no onKeyDown handler for keyboard accessibility. AFTER: For the dismiss button, add onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(toast.id); } }}. For the action button (if toast.action exists), add the same onKeyDown pattern calling toast.action.onClick. For focus visibility, add a state variable like [dismissFocused, setDismissFocused] (which already exists in the code) and wire onFocus={() => setDismissFocused(true)} and onBlur={() => setDismissFocused(false)} to the dismiss button. When dismissFocused is true, apply outline: '2px solid ' + colors.primary and outlineOffset: '2px' to the dismiss button style. Apply the same focus ring pattern to the action button using a separate focusState. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Procore's notification system uses persistent in-app banners with email fallback but lacks ARIA live regions on their toast system as well. Linear and Stripe both implement role='alert' with aria-live on all transient notifications, which is the standard SiteSync should match. Fieldwire's mobile breadcrumbs collapse elegantly with an ellipsis pattern on deep folder navigation, which is exactly what the FolderBreadcrumbs component needs.
summary: The UI Design System module provides a solid foundation with theme tokens, a toast notification system, and breadcrumb navigation, but has critical accessibility gaps that would fail WCAG 2.1 AA audit. Toast notifications are invisible to screen readers without ARIA live regions (the single highest impact fix). Mobile breadcrumb handling is incomplete with truncated logic, and the route label map is missing several construction-specific pages causing raw URL slugs to appear. Error toast auto-dismiss timing needs adjustment to give field users enough time to read failures. Keyboard interaction on toast action buttons needs focus ring styles since inline CSS cannot handle pseudo-selectors. All five issues are surgical fixes to existing components that will measurably improve accessibility scores, mobile usability, and interaction quality without any new feature work.
